title: Epitaph Of La Fontaine - Made By Himself
content:
JOHN, as he came, so went away,
    Consuming capital and pay,
    Holding superfluous riches cheap;
    The trick of spending time he knew,
    Dividing it in portions two,
    For idling one, and one for sleep.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jean de La Fontaine, titled "Epitaph Of La Fontaine - Made By Himself", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
